description: Game mechanics depth pass: four new systems to address deck power imbalance and add strategic depth. (1) Weak-side card rule forces every card to have at least one exploitable low side. (2) Mists intensity buff raises the randomness modifier from ±1 to ±2 to make the roll matter. (3) Plus rule (from Triple Triad) creates a side-value-matching capture that rewards card knowledge and makes filler cards strategically viable. (4) Elemental system assigns elements to cards and board cells, adding a positional layer (cell element match = +1 bonus). Includes backend rules engine changes, API updates, DB snapshot field additions, UI integration, and full documentation refresh.

userStories.title: Weak-side card rule: every card must have at least one side ≤ 3
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want every card — including ultra/very-rare — to have at least one exploitable low side so that filler cards can threaten powerful ones on at least one edge, breaking the race-to-high-stats dynamic.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a weak-side validation rule to `validate_card_balance` in `app/rules/cards.py`: reject any card where all four sides are ≥ 4 (i.e., min(sides) must be ≤ 3)
Audit all cards in `cards.jsonl`; for any card that violates the rule, reduce one side to ≤ 3 (prefer the thematically weakest direction; keep total sum within existing budget bounds where possible)
Update `CARDS_SPEC.md` to document the weak-side requirement under validation rules
Add or update unit tests in `tests/` covering: valid card with one side ≤ 3 passes; card with all sides ≥ 4 fails validation; existing card loader still rejects malformed cards
`uv run pytest` passes with no regressions
userStories.notes: Do not change tier/rarity budgets — only add the min-side floor. When adjusting cards.jsonl, prefer reducing the side that is already the card's lowest. Ultra cards likely violate this (avg side 8.0) and will need the biggest adjustment.

userStories.title: Mists intensity buff: change modifier from ±1 to ±2
userStories.description: As a player, I want the Mists of Barovia roll to feel impactful and risky so that each placement carries genuine tension. The ±1 modifier rarely flips outcomes given the stat spread; doubling it to ±2 makes Fog genuinely threatening and Omen genuinely exciting.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/rules/reducer.py`, change Fog (roll 1) modifier from -1 to -2 and Omen (roll 6) modifier from +1 to +2
Devout archetype behavior unchanged: it still negates Fog entirely (modifier → 0 regardless of magnitude); update any docstring/comment that says '-1' to say '-2'
Caster archetype behavior unchanged: it still rerolls once; the new ±2 magnitude increases its value naturally
Update `CLAUDE.md` Key Domain Concepts section: Mists now reads 'Fog (−2 all comparisons)' and 'Omen (+2)'
Update all backend tests that assert mists modifier values (look for hardcoded 1/-1); `uv run pytest` passes
Update any UI text/tooltip in `web/src/` that describes the Mists effect as ±1 to say ±2
`bun run test --run` passes
userStories.notes: This is a targeted one-number change in the reducer; the main work is finding and updating all test assertions and any UI strings that mention the old value.

userStories.title: Plus rule — backend: side-sum matching capture mechanic
userStories.description: As a player, I want a Plus capture rule so that specific side value combinations create unexpected flips, rewarding card knowledge and making lower-powered cards strategically interesting. Plus rule: when placing a card, if 2 or more adjacent opponent-owned cards each have (placed card's attacking side value + that neighbor's defending side value) equal to the same sum, all matching neighbors are captured — even if the placed card's side is lower.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Plus rule as a pre-step in `resolve_captures` in `app/rules/captures.py`: before standard comparison captures, compute the side-sum for each adjacent opponent-owned card; if 2+ share the same sum, capture all matching cards immediately (flip ownership), then enqueue each for the standard BFS combo chain
Plus uses raw printed side values only — Mists modifier and elemental bonus (from US-GM-006) do NOT apply to Plus sum calculation
After Plus captures are resolved, standard comparison captures still run for any remaining adjacent opponent cards not yet captured by Plus
Plus-captured cards enter the BFS combo queue with zero modifiers (same as normal combo behavior)

Add `plus_triggered: bool` field to `LastMoveInfo` in `app/models/game.py`; set to `True` when Plus fires, `False` otherwise; default to `False` for backward compat (existing snapshots without this field should deserialize safely)

New tests in `tests/test_gm003_plus_rule.py`: Plus triggers when 2+ sums match; no trigger with only 1 matching pair; Plus+combo chain propagates correctly; Plus and standard captures can coexist in same placement; Plus does not apply Mists/elemental to sum; `plus_triggered` is False when Plus does not fire
`uv run pytest` passes with no regressions
userStories.notes: Plus rule fires based on raw printed values. The 'sum' is: placed_card_side_value + neighbor_facing_side_value. Example: placing a card with N=6; cell 1 has S=7 → sum=13; cell 3 has E=10 (placing card's W=3) → W+E=13 → Plus triggers, both captured. Standard > comparison then still runs for any unfired neighbors.

userStories.title: Plus rule — UI: callout and capture feedback
userStories.description: As a player, I want clear visual feedback when the Plus rule fires so I understand the special capture and can learn to play toward it.
acceptanceCriteria: When `game.last_move.plus_triggered === true`, show a 'Plus!' callout in the capture feedback area (same region as existing amber/purple capture feedback from US-UX-010)
Plus! callout is visually distinct from standard capture feedback (use a different color, e.g. cyan/teal, and the word 'Plus!')
If Plus triggers AND there are standard captures in addition, show both indicators
Callout works in dark mode and on mobile
Update `web/src/lib/api.ts` `LastMoveInfo` interface to include `plus_triggered: boolean`
Add/update frontend tests covering: Plus! callout shown when plus_triggered=true; no callout when plus_triggered=false; Plus! and capture count can coexist
`bun run test --run` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GM-003. Reuse the existing capture feedback pattern established in US-UX-010; Plus! is an additive indicator, not a replacement.

userStories.title: Elemental system — card data and model
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want each card to carry an element so the elemental board system has something to match against. Elements are Barovia-themed and derived from each card's existing tags.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `element: str` field to `CardDefinition` in `app/models/cards.py`; valid values are exactly: 'blood', 'holy', 'arcane', 'shadow', 'nature'
Update `app/rules/cards.py` card loader to validate that `element` is one of the five valid values; missing or invalid element is a load error

Assign an element to every card in `cards.jsonl` using this thematic mapping: tags containing 'undead', 'vampire', or 'boss' (undead bosses) → blood; tags containing 'cleric', 'paladin', or 'devout' → holy; tags containing 'wizard', 'warlock', 'arcane', or 'spellcaster' → arcane; tags containing 'rogue', 'assassin', 'werewolf', or 'shadow' → shadow; tags containing 'ranger', 'druid', 'beast', 'wolf', or 'nature' → nature; for cards with no clear match use best thematic judgment (document each choice with a comment in the jsonl or via a clear rule in the PR notes)
Update `CARDS_SPEC.md`: document the element field, the five valid values, and the thematic assignment guidelines
Add unit tests: card with valid element loads; card with invalid element fails; card missing element fails
`uv run pytest` passes with no regressions
userStories.notes: cards.jsonl is JSON Lines — add 'element' key to each object. DO NOT guess element assignments from tags alone. Use `ralph/card_elements.json` as the authoritative lookup — it contains the correct element and a reason for all 65 cards. Apply exactly what is listed there.

userStories.title: Elemental system — game state, rules engine, and API
userStories.description: As a player, I want the board to have elemental cells so that where I play matters strategically. Placing a card on a matching element cell gives +1 to all its sides for that placement's initial comparisons.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `board_elements: list[str] | None` to `GameState` in `app/models/game.py` (None for backward compat with old snapshots)

In game creation (`services/` or equivalent), generate `board_elements` as a list of 9 elements (one per cell) using `random.Random(seed).choices(['blood','holy','arcane','shadow','nature'], k=9)` — deterministic from the game seed
In `app/rules/reducer.py`, before calling `resolve_captures`, compute `elemental_bonus = 1 if board_elements[cell_index] == placed_card.element else 0` (treat missing board_elements as 0)
Pass the combined modifier `mists_modifier + elemental_bonus` to `resolve_captures` as the existing modifier parameter (no new parameter needed — it stacks naturally)
Elemental bonus applies only to the placed card's initial comparisons; the BFS combo chain receives zero elemental bonus (same as Mists — combos use raw stats)
Elemental bonus does NOT apply to Plus rule sum calculations (Plus always uses raw printed values)
Backward compatibility: if `board_elements` is None or absent from a persisted snapshot, elemental bonus is 0 for all placements
`GET /games/:gameId` response includes `board_elements` (it is already part of GameState JSONB — verify the field serializes correctly)

New tests in `tests/test_gm006_elemental.py`: match gives +1 bonus and can flip a capture; mismatch gives no bonus; combo chain has no elemental bonus; Plus sums ignore elemental; old snapshot without board_elements works; board_elements present in GET response
`uv run pytest` passes with no regressions
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GM-005 for element field on CardDefinition. board_elements is stored in the JSONB snapshot — no DB schema migration needed. Generation happens once at game creation and is deterministic. The modifier stacking (mists + elemental) uses the existing single modifier parameter already passed to resolve_captures.

userStories.title: Elemental system — UI: board cell elements and match highlighting
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the element of each board cell and get a visual cue when my selected card matches a cell's element, so I can plan elemental bonus plays.
acceptanceCriteria: Each board cell displays a small element label or icon. Use short themed labels: Blood (🩸), Holy (✦), Arcane (✦ or ✧), Shadow (◆), Nature (✿) — or simple colored text tags if emoji are problematic in the font stack. Empty cells should still show their element.

When a hand card is selected, cells whose element matches the selected card's element are visually highlighted (e.g., a distinct ring/glow color, separate from the standard 'can place here' indicator)
When no card is selected, no highlight is shown (element labels still visible but no glow)
Update `web/src/lib/api.ts` `GameState` interface to include `board_elements: string[] | null`
Element display works in dark mode and on mobile without cluttering the board

Add frontend tests: element label renders on empty cell; element label renders on occupied cell; selected card highlights matching cells; no highlight when no card selected; non-matching cells not highlighted
`bun run test --run` passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GM-006. board_elements index maps directly to cell_index (0-8, row-major). BoardGrid already receives board state — add board_elements and selectedCardElement as props. Keep element indicators small so they don't compete with card name/stat display.

userStories.title: Game rules documentation refresh
userStories.description: As a developer or player reading the repo, I want all four new mechanics documented accurately so the rules are unambiguous and the codebase is self-explanatory.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `CLAUDE.md` Key Domain Concepts section: Mists now shows Fog (−2) and Omen (+2); add a 'Plus rule' entry with the sum-matching definition; add an 'Elemental' entry with the 5 elements, match=+1 rule, and note that Plus/combo ignore it
Update `CARDS_SPEC.md`: add weak-side rule (min side ≤ 3 required); add element field documentation with valid values and thematic mapping; update any budget/validation table that is now stale

If a standalone `GAME_RULES_OVERVIEW.md` exists, update it; if not, create a concise one in the repo root covering the full rule set (board, capture, Plus, Mists, Elemental, archetypes, Sudden Death) — 1-2 pages maximum
Update `AGENTS.md` if any commands changed (they likely have not, but verify)
All four mechanics are described with enough precision that a new developer could implement them from the docs alone
No code changes required for this story; docs only
userStories.notes: Do this last so the docs accurately reflect the final implemented behavior. Cross-check the implemented code against what the docs say — fix any discrepancies found.
